message:
feat(list): rich cell styling, semantic last-active tracking, and rich_rust Row/Cell migration

Migrate the session list table from add_row_cells (plain string slices)
to rich_rust's typed Row/Cell API, enabling per-cell Style application.
This unlocks three new visual enhancements:

1. Provider-aware cell styling: each provider name now renders with a
   unique bold+color style (e.g. claude-code = bold magenta, codex =
   bold cyan, gemini = bold yellow, etc.) via a new provider_style()
   helper that returns a parsed Style, replacing the old provider_display()
   which returned pre-colored String via the colored crate.  The new
   provider_display() is simplified to return &str (no allocation).

2. Message-count heat styling: a new message_count_style() helper assigns
   tiered bold colors based on conversation depth — bright_cyan for 200+
   messages, cyan for 50+, blue for 10+, and dim for fewer — giving an
   at-a-glance sense of session engagement.

3. Last-active age-based styling: a new last_active_style() helper colors
   the "Last Active" column based on recency — bright_green for <1h,
   green for <1d, yellow for <1w, magenta for <30d, and dim for older —
   making stale vs. hot sessions instantly distinguishable.

Additionally, the last-active timestamp computation is upgraded from
pure filesystem metadata (mtime/atime) to a new session_activity_millis()
function that prefers conversation-level timestamps (ended_at, max
message timestamp, started_at) and falls back to file mtime only when
conversation timestamps are unavailable.  This gives more accurate
recency tracking for providers whose session files are not updated
in-place (e.g. files that are written once then read).

The import line is updated to pull in Cell and Row from rich_rust::prelude,
and Column widths are adjusted for consistent alignment with the new
justify and width settings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
